# Kravn

> Kravn is a self-hostable, source-available enterprise MCP gateway, registry and proxy. It brings the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to your organization on your own infrastructure — integrated with your identity stack (SAML, OIDC, SCIM, RBAC), governed by your own policies, with no data ever leaving your perimeter.

Kravn sits between AI clients (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, your own agents) and the MCP servers and corporate systems they need. It connects to upstream MCP servers over streamable-HTTP, SSE or stdio, imports their tools/resources/prompts into one registry, and re-exposes them behind a single governed endpoint. It ships enterprise identity out of the box, governance pipelines (redact secrets/PII, prompt-injection guard, tamper-evident audit), and boots in one command. Built by AddLayer in TypeScript (Fastify + Vue 3). Licensed under BSL 1.1 (converts to Apache 2.0), free to self-host.

## Docs

- [Why Kravn](https://kravn.ai/guide/what-is-kravn): What Kravn is, the problem it solves, and its highlights.
- [Comparison](https://kravn.ai/comparison): How to choose an MCP gateway and how Kravn compares to SaaS tooling, self-hosted alternatives and IBM MCP Context Forge.
- [FAQ](https://kravn.ai/faq): Direct answers on licensing, self-hosting, identity, data privacy and installation.
- [Core concepts](https://kravn.ai/guide/concepts): Gateway, registry, MCP endpoints, pipelines, teams and entitlements.
- [Get started](https://kravn.ai/guide/getting-started): Run Kravn in one command.
- [Installation](https://kravn.ai/guide/installation): Docker and Helm installation manual.
- [Configuration](https://kravn.ai/guide/configuration): Runtime settings — SSRF policy, rate limits, transports, auth, observability.
- [Plugins & integrations](https://kravn.ai/guide/plugins): The plugin SDK and the catalog of 100+ MCP servers.
- [Security & compliance](https://kravn.ai/guide/security): Governance, audit, key management and licensing.
- [Integrations gallery](https://kravn.ai/integrations): The full catalog of ready-to-add MCP servers.

## Optional

- [Source on GitHub](https://github.com/addlayerio/kravn): The Kravn source repository.
- [License (BSL 1.1)](https://github.com/addlayerio/kravn/blob/main/LICENSE): Business Source License 1.1, converting to Apache 2.0.
